commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P1] The remaining action is maintainer/contributor proof and compatibility judgment, not a narrow code repair suitable for automation.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain issue found; the diff changes provider metadata, manifest parsing/types, docs, and tests without changing dependencies, workflows, secrets handling, or executable artifact s... (sha=7096e53ffb60d6595eeb723e27ffcf57d0663f19)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds provider-owned media-understanding capability overrides, registers Qwen media-understanding aliases, documents the manifest field, and updates resolver/tool tests so text-only Qwen chat models are not selected for image requests.

PR surface: Source +294, Tests +384, Docs +12. Total +690 across 29 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source: current main trusts configured model `input` metadata in defaults, registry, runner, image-tool, and PDF-tool paths, matching the linked Qwen/DashScope bad-routing report. I did not run a live DashScope/OpenClaw reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining action is maintainer/contributor proof and compatibility judgment, not a narrow code repair suitable for automation.

target.title: fix #92688: [Bug]: Qwen vision models fail with 400 "Unexpected item type in content" on DashScope
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

Root cause: OpenClaw's media-understanding resolver trusted user/catalog `input: ["text", "image"]` metadata as the final image-capability source of truth. That allowed a known text-only Qwen Cloud chat model such as `qwen3.7-max` to cross the image resolver contract and be routed into DashScope image requests, which caused `400 Unexpected item type in content` instead of selecting the provider's image-capable default.

Why does this matter now?

A bad routing decision loses the user's image-understanding request before the Qwen provider can return a useful result. The issue is user-visible because an otherwise valid Qwen setup can fail whenever a text-only max chat model is configured with overly broad image metadata.

What is the intended outcome?

Fix classification: root-cause fix. Provider-owned media-understanding metadata is now the canonical contract for model capability overrides, and Qwen-specific defaults/capability facts live in the Qwen plugin runtime/manifest instead of shared core production logic.

What is intentionally out of scope?

What did NOT change: this does not change Qwen auth, endpoint selection, API-key handling, request transport, or live DashScope behavior. It intentionally does not upgrade the Qwen image default to `qwen3.5-plus`; the default remains `qwen-vl-max-latest`. No unrelated provider defaults or migration behavior were changed.

What does success look like?

A configured text-only model like `qwen3.7-max` is rejected as an image model when provider-owned metadata marks it non-image, and OpenClaw falls back to the Qwen image default `qwen-vl-max-latest` instead of sending that chat model through the image path.

What should reviewers focus on?

Architecture / source-of-truth check: shared core only consumes the generic `modelCapabilityOverrides` contract. Qwen-specific provider aliases, default models, and `qwen3.7-max` non-image family facts are owned by the Qwen plugin and manifest metadata. This keeps the canonical provider contract at the plugin seam and prevents future Qwen capability drift from hard-coded shared-core mappings.

## Linked context

Closes #92688.

Related open PR scan: this refresh is scoped to #92782 / #92688 and the Qwen media-understanding contract. I did not find a reason to fold in any unrelated open PR behavior or broaden the public contract beyond provider-owned model capability overrides.

This refresh addresses the author-side review finding on #92782: Qwen-specific capability/default facts should be owned by the Qwen plugin/manifest, not hard-coded in shared core.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: a Qwen text-only chat model such as `qwen3.7-max` can be configured with `input: ["text", "image"]`, but should not be routed into the DashScope image request path when provider-owned metadata says the model is non-image.
- Real environment tested: DashScope OpenAI-compatible chat completions with a temporary API key in the earlier #92782 live probe; the secret value was not logged. This refresh also used the local OpenClaw workspace with the real media-understanding resolver, provider registry, manifest metadata registry, image tool/PDF tool config paths, and Qwen plugin registration path.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: sent redacted image chat-completion probes to DashScope compatible mode with image-capable Qwen models and the problematic `qwen3.7-max` model. `qwen3.5-plus` below is only an image-capable control model from that probe; this PR keeps the OpenClaw Qwen image default at `qwen-vl-max-latest`. Then ran the focused OpenClaw resolver/plugin regression suite shown below.
- Evidence after fix:

```text
Redacted DashScope live output, compatible-mode endpoint, API key not logged:

qwen3.5-plus + OpenAI-compatible image_url shape: HTTP 200, assistant text: 'red'
qwen3.7-plus + OpenAI-compatible image_url shape: HTTP 200, assistant text: 'red'
qwen-vl-max + OpenAI-compatible image_url shape: HTTP 200, assistant text: 'red'

qwen3.7-max + OpenAI-compatible image_url shape: HTTP 400
Error preview: Unexpected item type in content

qwen3.7-max + native image shape: HTTP 400
Error preview: The current model only supports text modality and does not support image input.

DashScope native image block shape with image-capable Qwen models: HTTP 400
Error preview: Invalid value: image. Supported values are: 'text','image_url','video_url' and 'video'.
```

- Exact OpenClaw command run after this patch refresh:

```bash
node scripts/test-projects.mjs \
  src/plugins/manifest-registry.test.ts \
  src/media-understanding/defaults.test.ts \
  src/media-understanding/provider-registry.test.ts \
  src/media-understanding/provider-capability-registry.test.ts \
  src/media-understanding/runner.vision-skip.test.ts \
  src/agents/tools/image-tool.test.ts \
  src/agents/tools/pdf-tool.model-config.test.ts \
  src/media-understanding/image.test.ts \
  extensions/qwen/index.test.ts \
  extensions/qwen/media-understanding-provider.test.ts \
  --testNamePattern "mediaUnderstandingProviderMetadata|bundled image defaults|bundled video fallback|Qwen text-only|provider-owned metadata|plugin-owned model overrides|configured Qwen max chat|Qwen max chat metadata|known non-image Qwen|media-understanding aliases|qwen video|Qwen video|Qwen Cloud media"
```

- OpenClaw evidence after this patch refresh:

```text
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.media-understanding.config.ts

 Test Files  5 passed (5)
      Tests  7 passed | 60 skipped (67)

[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts

 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  2 passed | 91 skipped (93)

[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.plugins.config.ts

 Test Files  1 skipped (1)
      Tests  70 skipped (70)

[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.extension-providers.config.ts

 Test Files  1 passed | 1 skipped (2)
      Tests  1 passed | 8 skipped (9)

[test] passed 4 Vitest shards in 54.25s
```

- Observed result after fix: live DashScope output confirms `qwen3.7-max` is not a usable image model while image-capable Qwen models accept the OpenAI-compatible `image_url` payload. Current OpenClaw resolver coverage now rejects provider-owned text-only image metadata and falls back to the provider image default. The Qwen plugin registers `qwen`, `qwencloud`, `modelstudio`, and `dashscope` media-understanding providers with `qwen-vl-max-latest` as the image default and `qwen3.7-max` as a non-image family.
- What was not tested: no full interactive OpenClaw gateway session was rerun in this environment, and the copied live DashScope probe did not cover every Qwen model alias or every regional/auth mode.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: this refresh environment does not include a live Qwen/DashScope API key, so the new validation proves the current OpenClaw routing decision that prevents the bad DashScope image request, while the copied redacted live provider output proves the underlying DashScope model/payload behavior.
- Before evidence: the RED test for provider-owned metadata previously selected the configured text-only model (`atlas-chat`) as an image model instead of falling back to the provider image default. The Qwen alias registration RED test also showed only `qwen` registered before this refresh; the aliases are now registered with provider-owned metadata.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

Target test file: focused regression coverage was run through `node scripts/test-projects.mjs` across media-understanding, agents tool config, plugin manifest, and Qwen extension provider test shards. After remote CI exposed the missing registration-contract expectation, the failing contract was reproduced locally and the full contracts-plugin shard was rerun. Type coverage was also run with `pnpm tsgo:core` and `pnpm tsgo:extensions`. This refresh also documents the new manifest `modelCapabilityOverrides` contract and validates the updated manifest docs.

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs --config test/vitest/vitest.contracts-plugin.config.ts src/plugins/contracts/plugin-registration.contract.test.ts -t "qwen plugin registration contract"
```

```text
Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  3 passed | 87 skipped (90)
```

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs --config test/vitest/vitest.contracts-plugin.config.ts
```

```text
Test Files  43 passed (43)
     Tests  953 passed (953)
```

```bash
pnpm tsgo:core
```

```text
$ node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo
```

```bash
pnpm tsgo:extensions
```

```text
$ node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.extensions.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions.tsbuildinfo
```

```bash
pnpm exec node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs/plugins/manifest.md
```

```text
Docs MDX check passed (1 files, 439ms).
```

```bash
pnpm format:docs:check
```

```text
$ node scripts/format-docs.mjs --check
Docs formatting clean (662 files).
```

```bash
pnpm lint:docs
```

```text
markdownlint-cli2 v0.22.1 (markdownlint v0.40.0)
Finding: docs/**/*.md docs/**/*.mdx README.md !docs/zh-CN/** !docs/.i18n/** !docs/reference/templates/** !**/.local/**
Linting: 661 file(s)
Summary: 0 error(s)
```

What regression coverage was added or updated?

Scenario locked in: configured text-only model metadata is no longer enough to make a model image-capable when provider-owned metadata says otherwise. Coverage also locks that Qwen aliases receive the same plugin-owned metadata and that only canonical `qwen` contributes video auto-priority, preserving the default video fallback order.

What failed before this fix, if known?

Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the first provider-owned metadata RED test selected the configured text-only model (`atlas-chat`) instead of the provider image default (`atlas-vision`). The Qwen alias registration RED test showed that only `qwen` was registered before this refresh. These targeted tests cover the routing contract directly without requiring a live API key.

If no test was added, why not?

Focused tests were added/updated for the resolver, registries, manifest metadata, image/PDF tool paths, and Qwen plugin registration.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? `Yes`

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? `No`

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? `No`

What is the highest-risk area?

Risk labels considered: compatibility and auth-provider. The highest-risk area is the public config/provider model-selection contract, because this patch changes how configured `input` metadata is interpreted when provider-owned capability overrides exist.

Risk explanation: the compatibility risk is intentional and narrow: a model configured as image-capable can now be ignored for image routing only when the provider's own metadata marks it non-image. The auth-provider risk is limited to provider metadata registration; this patch does not change tokens, credentials, endpoint classes, request headers, or API-key resolution.

How is that risk mitigated?

Why acceptable: the generic contract makes provider-owned metadata the canonical boundary, keeps Qwen-specific facts out of shared core, and preserves the existing Qwen default `qwen-vl-max-latest`. Patch quality warnings around fallback/default language are not masking the symptom; they describe the intended resolver behavior after the source-of-truth contract rejects a text-only model.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Maintainer-ready confidence: high after focused resolver/plugin validation and type checks. Next action is maintainer review and CI on the refreshed head.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

The latest re-review asked for manifest docs and maintainer-visible real provider behavior proof. This refresh adds the missing `modelCapabilityOverrides` manifest docs, restores copied redacted DashScope provider output in the PR body, and keeps the current OpenClaw routing validation tied to the submitted head. The remaining work after submit is maintainer/CI review of the refreshed head.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

- RF-001 fixed: `docs/plugins/manifest.md` now documents `modelCapabilityOverrides`, including exact `nonImageModels` and family-based `nonImageModelFamilies` semantics.
- RF-002 fixed/disclosed: the PR body now includes copied redacted live DashScope output showing image-capable Qwen `image_url` success and `qwen3.7-max` image rejection; the refresh environment itself did not rerun a live API call because no Qwen/DashScope key is available.
- RF-003 fixed/disclosed: the real behavior proof section now combines copied live DashScope provider output with current OpenClaw resolver/plugin validation that proves this head routes away from text-only image metadata.
- RF-004 fixed: the additive public plugin manifest metadata contract is documented in the manifest reference.
- RF-005 fixed/disclosed: endpoint-level behavior is represented by copied redacted DashScope output, while current-head OpenClaw validation proves the routing behavior added by this PR.
- RF-006 resolved for review: the compatibility-sensitive behavior is documented as provider-owned metadata overriding only image routing for models the provider marks non-image; auth, endpoint, transport, and other capabilities are unchanged.
- RF-007 fixed/disclosed: contributor-side proof and docs work are complete in this refresh; maintainer judgment remains for merge approval.
- RF-008 fixed: `docs/plugins/manifest.md` documents the new manifest capability override surfaced from `src/plugins/manifest.ts`.
- RF-009 fixed locally: the Qwen plugin registration contract was reproduced and the full contracts-plugin shard passed.
- RF-010 fixed locally: media-understanding resolver/registry/defaults/runner/image tests passed in focused validation.
- RF-011 fixed locally: image tool and PDF tool configured-model path tests passed in focused validation.
- RF-012 fixed locally: `pnpm tsgo:core` passed on the refreshed patch.
- RF-013 fixed locally: `pnpm tsgo:extensions` passed on the refreshed patch.

Patch quality notes: the diff touches multiple resolver entry points because the same source-of-truth boundary is consumed by defaults, runner selection, image runtime, image tool config, PDF tool config, provider registry, capability registry, and manifest metadata. Splitting those consumers would leave some user paths still able to route text-only models into image requests.
target.author: zhangguiping-xydt

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
